entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop evaluated for resistance to wilt and root rot.

entities.name: WILT
entities.description: A disease affecting chickpea, caused by Fusarium oxysporum f.sp. ciceris.

entities.name: ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting chickpea roots, caused by various fungal pathogens.

entities.name: MADHYA PRADESH
entities.description: An Indian state from which chickpea accessions were collected.

entities.name: RAJASTHAN
entities.description: An Indian state from which chickpea accessions were collected.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country from which chickpea accessions were collected.

entities.name: BANGLADESH
entities.description: Country from which chickpea accessions were collected.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, where the evaluation was conducted.

entities.name: JG62
entities.description: A susceptible chickpea cultivar used as a comparison in the study.

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F.SP. CICERIS
entities.description: A fungal pathogen responsible for causing wilt in chickpea.

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen responsible for causing root rot in chickpea.

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen responsible for causing root rot in chickpea.

entities.name: FUSARIUM SOLANI
entities.description: A fungal pathogen responsible for causing root rot in chickpea.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea accessions to survive and maintain yield despite disease pressure.

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The percentage of plant death used to quantify disease susceptibility.

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The combined weight of 100 seeds, used as a morphological trait.

entities.name: FLOWERING TIME
entities.description: The period when the plant flowers, used as a phenological trait.

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of a chickpea line to produce grain yield per unit area.

entities.name: ANNIGERI
entities.description: A high-yielding chickpea cultivar used as a control.

entities.name: ICC ENTRIES
entities.description: Specific chickpea accessions identified as sources of resistance and high yield.
